checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: ProfitLoss=-56333000 (2012 Q1), ProfitLoss=92900000 (2012 Q2), ProfitLoss=242900000 (2012 Q3), quarterly ProfitLoss=-36800000 implied (2012 Q4 residual)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filing_date=2014-03-03 followed by 10-K filing_date=2014-03-19 and 10-K/A filing_date=2014-03-20
8-K filing_date=2014-03-18 immediately preceding the delayed 10-K and same-day-amended 10-K/A
NT 10-K filing_date=2013-03-04 filed the same day as 10-K filing_date=2013-03-04
evidence.location: filing_chronology entries NT 10-K and 10-K both dated 2013-03-04

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filing_date=2009-03-03 filed the same day as 10-K filing_date=2009-03-03
evidence.location: filing_chronology entries NT 10-K and 10-K both dated 2009-03-03 (as referenced in historical chronology)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, CostOfRevenue/CostOfGoodsSold, or self-insurance reserve concepts were provided in the financial_series_point_in_time dataset, limiting CL4 and CL6 assessments; NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities/InvestingActivities data stops after Q1 2011.

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Restatement of previously issued financial statements for FY2011-FY2013 (and interim 2013 periods), evidenced by a 10-K/A filed only one day after the original 10-K, preceded by an NT 10-K notifying of late filing. This pattern is consistent with correction of errors related to expense recognition/capitalization timing that had inflated reported net income in the original filings before restatement.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic quarterly components filed pre-restatement: -132600000 (2011 Q1) + 55000000 (2011 Q2) + 206700000 (2011 Q3) + 47000000 (2011 Q4) = 176,100,000, vs restated annual 164,200,000

affected_line_items: Allowance for doubtful accounts (not separately disclosed)
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential under-reserving or overstatement of trade/vehicle receivables in 2011-2012, corrected in the 2013 restated balance where AR fell sharply even as revenue grew strongly, suggesting a write-down or reserve catch-up embedded in the restatement.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=1879700000 (2012-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNet=1512600000 (2013-12-31) while Revenues rose from 9024900000 (2012) to 10771900000 (2013)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase price allocation from the Dollar Thrifty acquisition (closed Q4 2012) drove a large, rapid step-up in goodwill and indefinite-lived intangibles; risk that acquisition accounting estimates (fair value allocations) were aggressive, with impairment risk not yet reflected as of the data cutoff.

top_signals: A 10-K/A was filed only one day after the original 10-K (10-K 2014-03-19, 10-K/A 2014-03-20, accession 0001364479-14-000008), restating multi-year financial data for FY2011-FY2013, following an NT 10-K late-filing notice on 2014-03-03 and an 8-K on 2014-03-18 - a strong indicator of a material restatement.

Discrepancies exist between net income figures reported in pre-restatement quarterly filings (e.g., 2011 quarterly NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic summing to 176,100,000) versus the restated annual figure (164,200,000) in the 10-K/A.

Accounts receivable grew substantially faster than revenue in 2011-2012 (AR +19.2% vs revenue +9.7% in 2011; AR +16.3% vs revenue +8.7% in 2012) then fell sharply in 2013 even as revenue grew +19.4%, consistent with prior overstatement later corrected.
Large, rapid increases in goodwill and intangible assets in Q4 2012 tied to the Dollar Thrifty acquisition raise purchase-accounting and future impairment risk.

Persistent gap between net income/loss and operating cash flow across all years is largely explainable by the capital-intensive, depreciation-heavy rental fleet business model, but combined with the restatement evidence it compounds overall reporting-quality concerns.
Repeated NT 10-K filings (2009, 2013, 2014) indicate recurring difficulty meeting filing deadlines.
